data.title: Stock index futures rise as softer inflation data keep sentiments high
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday as the S&P 500 hoped to extend its four-day straight rally, while investors awaited the consumer sentiment report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.2%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.41%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 3.95%.

Wall Street on Thursday ended mixed, but its benchmark S&P 500 index extended a rally to four straight days and further solidified itself in positive territory for the year.

"Markets continued to advance over the last 24 hours, as weak U.S. inflation data and lower oil prices raised expectations that the Federal Reserve would still cut rates this year," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

There were several factors driving the rally, but a critically important one was the U.S. PPI data for April. It showed inflation pressures were much softer than expected, Allen added.

The April producer price index (PPI) report showed both headline and core producer prices unexpectedly declining on a M/M basis.

On the economic side, the May consumer sentiment is expected to come in at 53.1.

The import/export prices report will also land on Friday.

data.title: Yatsen Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.01, revenue of $114.9M; initiates Q2 outlook and new share buyback program
data.description:
• Yatsen Holding press release (NYSE:YSG): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.01.
  • Revenue of $114.9M (+7.8% Y/Y).
  • As of March 31, 2025, the company had cash and short-term investments of RMB1.28 billion (US$176.4 million), as compared with RMB1.36 billion as of December 31, 2024.

  • Net cash generated from operating activities for the first quarter of 2025 was RMB23.8 million (US$3.3 million), as compared with net cash used in operating activities of RMB121.8 million for the prior year period.

  • On May 16, 2025, the company's board of directors approved a new share repurchase program, under which the company may repurchase up to US$30.0 million worth of its ordinary shares (including in the form of ADSs) over the following 24 months commencing on May 16, 2025. 

  • Business Outlook: For the second quarter of 2025, the Company expects its total net revenues to be between RMB810.4 million and RMB889.9 million, representing a year-over-year increase of approximately 2% to 12%. These forecasts reflect the Company's current and preliminary views on the market and operational conditions, which are subject to change.

data.title: NWTN gets new chief executive
data.description:
• NWTN  (NASDAQ:NWTN) appointed Mr. Benjamin Zhai as executive director of the Board and global CEO.
  • This leadership change marks a milestone in NWTN's transformation from a NEV manufacturer to a global smart technology platform, poised to revolutionize innovation and supply chain ecosystem.
  • He was former Chairman of NIO Global User Trust, CEO of Canoo (Go EV) China, CEO of Shenzhen Talent Group, Managing Partner of Russell Reynolds' Automotive Practice in Asia Pacific and Americas, Managing Partner of Egon Zehnder's Industrial Practice in Asia Pacific.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: SPCE, AMAT, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher while Treasury yields slipped in Friday’s premarket session, as the market's focus turned to today's options expirations.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Virgin Galactic (NYSE:SPCE) +15% — Shares climbed after the space tourism company posted better-than-expected Q1 results and reaffirmed its timeline for upcoming spaceflights. The first mission with its new Spaceship carrying research payloads remains on track for summer 2026, with private astronaut flights scheduled for fall 2026. CEO Michael Colglazier emphasized the long-term value of assets being developed during the company’s pre-revenue phase, projecting a scalable and profitable business model supported by cost efficiencies and a premium customer experience.
  • TSS (NASDAQ:TSSI) +56% — Shares soared after the firm posted Q1 results with revenue surging 523% Y/Y and diluted EPS rising to $0.12 from just above breakeven. Adjusted EBITDA increased more than tenfold from the prior year. CEO Darryll Dewan expressed optimism for 2025 and beyond, citing the launch of production at a new facility as a key growth driver. He reaffirmed expectations for H1 2025 revenue to exceed H2 2024 levels and projected FY2025 adjusted EBITDA to be at least 50% higher than 2024. The CEO also highlighted the new facility’s significant power capacity as a differentiator in the evolving AI landscape.

Biggest stock losers

  • Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) -6% — Shares fell after the semiconductor equipment maker posted mixed FQ2 results, with revenue missing estimates despite 7% Y/Y growth. For FQ3, the company guided revenue between $6.7B and $7.7B, with the $7.2B midpoint roughly in line with the $7.19B consensus. It forecasts an adjusted gross margin of 48.3% and EPS between $2.15 and $2.55, with the midpoint of $2.35 slightly above the $2.31 consensus.
  • Take-Two Interactive (NASDAQ:TTWO) -3% — Shares fell after the company posted mixed FQ4 results and issued a disappointing FY2026 outlook. Management guided for a full-year loss of $2.45 to $2.79 per share on revenue of $5.95B to $6.05B, far below analyst expectations of $6.89 EPS and $7.81B in revenue. Net bookings are projected between $5.9B and $6B. For FQ1, the company expects a per-share loss of $0.65 to $0.78, vs. consensus for a profit of $0.36, while revenue guidance of $1.35B to $1.4B topped expectations of $1.28B.

data.title: Eutelsat Communications reports Q3 results; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Eutelsat Communications press release (OTCPK:EUTLF): Q3 Revenue of €300M (-0.3% Y/Y).
  • The backlog stood at €3.6 billion as of 31 March 2025 versus €3.9 billion a year ago, and €3.7 billion at end-December 2024, reflecting its natural erosion, especially in the Video segment, in the absence of major renewals.

  • OUTLOOK

    On the back of the performance of the first Nine Months, we confirm our objectives for the Full Year 2024-25 of Operating Vertical Revenues around the same level as FY 20245 and adjusted EBITDA margin slightly below the level of FY 20246 .

    Elsewhere, gross capital expenditure in FY 2024-25 remains expected in a range of €500-600 millions.

    Eutelsat also continues to target leverage of c.3x in the medium term.
